title: quercus glauca subsp. euglauca
intro: Quercus glauca subsp. euglauca, also known as the Japanese blue oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its blue-green foliage and broad, rounded crown. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Quercus glauca subsp. euglauca include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, allowing for the extraction of its active compounds.
